findings.implication: Applicant CV: shows degrees in nutrition/dietetics and progressive research roles, supporting relevant research experience.
Applicant CV: lists 6 publications and multiple awards, demonstrating impactful outputs.
Applicant CV: career progression from Band 5 to Band 8a and lead applicant role indicate increasing research leadership.
Applicant CV: clinical and research roles in renal dietetics evidence practitioner-academic involvement.
Applicant Research Background: shows clear career vision and trajectory from clinical roles to aspiring clinical academic, with strong mentorship support.
Applicant Research Background: explicitly states reaching limits of current role and need for DCAF award to progress, building on prior experience.
Applicant Research Background: demonstrates high-quality previous research outputs including publication in Clinical Nutrition, grants, and supervision of MSc students.
Applicant Research Background: mentions supervisory support from Prof Fiona Jones and Prof Heather Jarman, and institutional support via TACRI.
Applicant Research Background: describes strengthening PPI through meetings with KRUK and RSS and lay review of proposal.
Plain English Summary of Research: Clear problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact; good readability and low jargon.
Plain English Summary of Research: Emphasizes patient benefits including reduced hospitalisation, improved quality of life, and alignment with NHS goals.
Plain English Summary of Research: Explicitly uses co-design and participatory methods with patients and nurses; includes feedback mechanisms.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract mentions prior tool cited in guidelines and used worldwide, indicating impactful prior output.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract describes three studies with explicit methods, sample size, and validation approach.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract states earlier detection, cost-effectiveness, improved patient care, supporting impact.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract explicitly uses co-design with nurses and patients.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract describes prior validated tool, showing prior successful project.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract includes indices of social deprivation and communication barriers, indicating inclusive design.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract describes codesign with patients and nurses.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract links study to earlier detection, cost savings, and improved care.
Scientific Abstract: Abstract mentions recording social deprivation and communication barriers, targeting underserved groups.
Detailed Research Plan: Section provides extensive literature review, critical analysis of existing tools (MUST not valid), and identifies knowledge gaps (lack of nurse/patient perspective).
Detailed Research Plan: Clearly defined three studies with timeline, sample size justification, success criteria, and risk management, indicating feasible scope for a fellowship award.
Detailed Research Plan: Inclusion considered throughout: codesign to explore barriers, diverse recruitment targets, hybrid participation options, and ongoing PPI integration.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section details multiple PPI consultations directly informing study design and revisions.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section describes dissemination plans and patient benefit through innovative co-design.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section explicitly addresses diversity via recruitment, barrier evaluation, and accessibility options.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section describes PPI involvement throughout planning, scoping review, co-design, analysis, and reporting.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section mentions reimbursement and training for online access, indicating costed inclusion resources.
Patient & Public Involvement: Section provides strong evidence of co-design, PPI review, and patient involvement at multiple stages.

Training & Development and Research Support: Proposed training programme is comprehensive, structured across 3 years, and directly relevant to project methods (scoping review, qualitative, co-design, implementation).
Training & Development and Research Support: Training is specifically mapped to project phases and personal skill gaps (qualitative, co-design, implementation).
Training & Development and Research Support: Training explicitly supports career progression to clinical academic leader with international collaboration.
Training & Development and Research Support: Host organisation has strong track record in NIHR clinical academic training and research.
Training & Development and Research Support: Supervisory team has relevant expertise, structured meetings, and strong commitment.
Training & Development and Research Support: Host provides physical resources, training infrastructure, and strategic alignment with career.
Training & Development and Research Support: Organisation demonstrates strong commitment to EDI and inclusive research culture.
Training & Development and Research Support: Training aims articulate clear career vision towards clinical academic leadership.
Training & Development and Research Support: Training and host support explicitly foster research leadership trajectory.
Training & Development and Research Support: Applicant maintains clinical practice and host supports practitioner-academic career.
SUMMARY BUDGET: provides explicit links between tasks and resources, with clear justifications for salary, travel, equipment, and PPI costs.
SUMMARY BUDGET: includes costed PPI and translation/interpreters budgets for inclusive research.

Plain English NLP Analysis: NLP metrics indicate good readability (Flesch-Kincaid Grade 10.05, Reading Ease 51.02, no long sentences), low jargon proxy density (2.35%), coverage of problem/objectives/methods/beneficiarie
